_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Recurring gout affecting several joints (polyarticular gout) or chronic deforming arthritis.
Urate deposition with serum uric acid levels greater than 6.8 mg/dL [404.5 mcmol/L].
More than 1 attack in last 12 months, often accompanied by fever up to 39°C.
Clinical attack of gout or inflamed tophi present.
Acute, monarticular arthritis, often of the first MTP joint, with common recurrence.
Identification of urate crystals in joint fluid or tophi is diagnostic.

clinical_thresholds.note: Urate deposition occurs when serum uric acid is supersaturated.

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment

claim.label: Detailed indoor case papers along with indications

ppd_preauth.q: Urate deposition levels greater than 6.8 mg/dL [404.5 mcmol/L]?
Were Septic Arthritis, pseudogout, Rheumatoid Arthritis, hypertension, and impaired renal function causes been ruled out?

common_queries: Serum Uric acid levels not submitted at pre-authorization.
C-reactive protein level not submitted at pre-authorization.
Urate deposition levels not documented as greater than 6.8 mg/dL [404.5 mcmol/L] or not justified.
Other causes of inflammation like Septic Arthritis, pseudogout, Rheumatoid Arthritis, hypertension, or impaired renal function not explicitly ruled out.
Detailed indoor case papers and indications missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Detailed procedure notes missing or incomplete at claim submission.

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/ DNB/equivalent in General Medicine; DM in Rheumatology
clinical_key_pointers: Proceed with management of Gout only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs:
Recurring gout affecting several joints (called polyarticular gout) or chronic deforming arthritis.
Urate deposition occurs when serum uric acid is supersaturated (ie, at levels greater than 6.8 mg/dL [404.5 mcmol/L]).
If more than 1 attack in last 12 months and Fever is common and may reach 39°C.
If clinical attack of gout and tophi/ inflamed tophi.
It may develop without apparent precipitating cause or may follow rapid increases or decreases in serum urate levels.
The MTP joint of the great toe is the most susceptible joint (“podagra”).

Exclude other causes of inflammation, such as sepsis within the joint, especially if gout has not been previously diagnosed, calcium pyrophosphate crystal deposition, including pseudogout, Rheumatoid Arthritis.
Patients with gout typically have hypertension and impaired renal function.
Acute, monarticular arthritis, often of the first MTP joint; recurrence is common.
Polyarticular involvement more common in patients with longstanding disease.
Identification of urate crystals in joint fluid or tophi is diagnostic.
With chronicity, urate deposits in subcutaneous tissue, bone, cartilage, joints, and other tissues.
Identification of negatively birefringent sodium urate crystals in joint fluid or material aspirated from a tophus.
additional_information.comorbidity_management: Patients with gout typically have hypertension and impaired renal function. Consideration of renal and hepatic insufficiency when prescribing medication. Pharmacological management for chronic kidney disease.
contraindications: Sepsis within the joint (if not previously diagnosed as gout)
